title: Universal ERC20 return data verification (Standard + Missing Returns)
explanation: This block safely evaluates ERC20 interactions across all implementations. First, it checks the standard: did the call succeed and return `1` (`true`)? If not, it falls back to checking non-standard implementations: it verifies that `returndatasize()` is exactly 0 (handling tokens like USDT) and crucially guarantees that `extcodesize(token)` is greater than 0, preventing the EVM from silently returning success on empty EOA addresses.
when_to_apply: Building low-level token interactions (Vaults, DEXs, Bridges) that cannot trust the implementation standards of the deployed ERC20s.
